description: Find ProgramDescriptor examples
Find reader kernel examples

Existing code is a staged TDD draft handling only pinned shape (1,1,128,128). Need to add: multi-block Q/KV loops, multi-core distribution, GQA/MQA head replication, mask handling, proper O accumulation and l_i update, output normalization. Key issues: Stage 11 add is missing, Stage 12 O+=PV add is missing, uses cb_sum_new instead of cb_sum_old for normalization, single-block only, single-core only.

error: Hang at Stage 12 PV matmul (compute_kernel.cpp:546). TRISC0 stuck at matmul_block in1_buf.wait_front(cb_v), TRISC2 stuck at reserve_back(cb_o_accum). Writer stuck at cb_wait_front(cb_out). Reader stuck at wait_for_brisc_notification. Root cause: cb_v has 2*D_t pages (8) but PV matmul needs B_kv_t*D_t=16 tiles in one K-block. cb_o_accum has B_q_t*D_t=16 pages but matmul tries to reserve row_group_tiles for TileRowMajor.

description: Rewrite all kernels for full Flash Attention. Key design decisions: (1) Use SubblockMajor tile_order for matmul (simpler CB layout, avoids TileRowMajor row_group reserve issues). (2) PV matmul with packer_l1_acc=false, num_k_blocks=1 - output to cb_o_accum, then eltwise add into cb_o. (3) For the O accumulation, use a separate cb_o_accum for PV matmul output, then add to cb_o in-place. (4) Properly handle scaler CB: reader pushes 2 scaler tiles, compute pops after each reduce. (5) Handle multi-block Q/KV loops, multi-core, GQA, mask.

error: Hang at compute_kernel.cpp:220 (add<cb_sum_old, cb_sum_new, cb_sum_old>). TRISC0 stuck in BinaryFpu wait_per_tile on cb_sum_old(30) or cb_sum_new(29). Phase 11 add: cb_sum_old was used in-place in phase 7 mul, then phase 11 tries to read it again with Streaming. The in-place mul in phase 7 consumed cb_sum_old tiles (Streaming pop), but the in-place add in phase 11 needs to wait for new tiles to be pushed. The issue: phase 7 pops cb_sum_old (in-place mul), leaving it empty. Phase 11 add tries to read cb_sum_old which is empty.

description: CB lifecycle issue: Phase 7 mul<cb_sum_old, cb_alpha, cb_sum_old> is in-place Streaming — it pops tiles and pushes them back. But the in-place chain pops BEFORE pushing, so after phase 7, cb_sum_old has B_q_t tiles at the front again (the rescaled values). Phase 11 add<cb_sum_old, cb_sum_new, cb_sum_old> should work: it reads from both, pops both, pushes to cb_sum_old. BUT: the chain might not handle in-place correctly when the same CB is both input A and output. Need to verify the chain handles pop-before-reserve correctly. Actually, the real issue may be that phase 7 used HeldBulk for cb_alpha but then phase 11 uses Streaming for cb_alpha. Phase 7: mul<cb_sum_old, cb_alpha, cb_sum_old, None, Streaming, Streaming>. This pops both cb_sum_old and cb_alpha. So cb_alpha is empty after phase 7. Phase 11: add<cb_sum_old, cb_sum_new, cb_sum_old>. This reads cb_sum_old (which has tiles from phase 7 rescale) and cb_sum_new (which has tiles from phase 10 reduce). Both should have tiles. The hang is TRISC0 waiting on wait_per_tile for BinaryFpu with CBs 30 and 29. Let me check if cb_sum_new actually has tiles pushed by the reduce.

The hang is at Phase 11 add. Let me check if cb_sum_new actually has tiles. The reduce<SUM, WaitUpfrontNoPop> should push B_q_t tiles. But maybe the issue is that the SUM reduce uses the matmul path (SUM REDUCE_ROW uses matmul), and the matmul state was left dirty by Phase 9 unary<Exp>. The reduce helper with reconfig_mode=INPUT_AND_OUTPUT (default) should reconfig. But maybe the matmul init for the reduce is not happening correctly after the eltwise chain. Let me also check: after Phase 4 reduce<MAX>, I pop the MAX scaler. Then Phase 5 eltwise_chain (alpha) runs. Then Phases 6-9 eltwise. Then Phase 10 reduce<SUM>. The reduce<SUM> needs to wait for cb_scaler_reduce. After popping MAX scaler, the SUM scaler should be at front. But Phase 10 reduce does scaler_dfb.wait_front(1) — it waits for 1 tile. The SUM scaler should be there. Let me try removing the scaler pop after Phase 4 and see if that helps — maybe the pop is causing an issue.

error: static_assert in BinaryFpu: (BIndex, BPolicy) illegal for Block. Root cause: Phase 8 sub uses OperandKind::Col with InputLifecycle::HeldStream, but Col requires non-draining lifecycles (Bulk/HeldBulk). Fix: use HeldBulk instead of HeldStream for cb_max_new in Phase 8.

description: Read helper headers
Analyze CB sync issues
findings: F1: Phase 11 outputs to cb_sum_new instead of cb_sum_old (revert to in-place). F2: Scaler tiles prepared once but consumed per KV-block (need re-push). F3: Writer total_tiles includes num_q_blocks but compute only outputs on last Q-block. Also: B_q_t/B_kv_t are fixed at 4 but test shapes like (1,1,32,32) have only 1 tile — reader reads out-of-bounds tiles.

description: Check packer_l1_acc semantics

Root cause for basic test hang: B_Q_T=4 and B_KV_T=4 are fixed, but test shape (1,1,32,32) has only 1 tile in each dim. Reader reads out-of-bounds tiles. Fix: make B_Q_T and B_KV_T dynamic — min(4, S_q_tiles) and min(4, S_kv_tiles). Pass actual block sizes as runtime args. Also fix: (1) Phase 11 revert to in-place on cb_sum_old. (2) Reader re-push scalers per KV-block. (3) Writer total_tiles = num_work_units * num_o_tiles (not * num_q_blocks). (4) Compute writes output every Q-block.
Read helper header signatures
Find split_work_to_cores usage
Check reduce helper scaler handling
Check reduce scaler pop and fill helpers

Full rewrite of all 3 kernels + program descriptor. Key design decisions: (1) Reader streams Q-block then K/V/mask blocks per KV iteration, handles GQA head mapping. (2) Compute: 13-phase flash attention recurrence with proper CB lifecycle for each phase. Key issues to fix: scaler CB needs separate handling for MAX (row-0 fill) vs SUM (col-0 fill) reduce — reader prepares fresh scaler before each reduce. Phase 5 eltwise_chain for alpha must use HeldBulk for both m_old and m_new since Col broadcast needs non-draining lifecycle. Phase 6 mul O*=alpha uses Col broadcast — alpha needs HeldBulk. Phase 8 sub S-=m_new uses Col broadcast — m_new needs HeldBulk. Phase 11 add l_i+=l_blk: output to cb_sum_old in-place is problematic if cb_sum_old was Streaming-popped in phase 7. Solution: phase 7 mul outputs to cb_sum_old in-place Streaming (pop+push), phase 11 reads from cb_sum_old Streaming (pop) and cb_sum_new Streaming (pop), outputs to cb_sum_old (push). Phase 12 PV matmul outputs to cb_o_accum, then add cb_o += cb_o_accum. Writer drains cb_out after normalization.

error: static_assert: OperandKind::Block is illegal with InputLifecycle::Streaming in mul<cb_o, cb_sum_old, cb_out, Col>

description: OperandKind::Block cannot be used with InputLifecycle::Streaming. Change to OperandKind::Scalar for the first operand in mul calls with Col broadcast.

error: 1) reconfig template param in wrong position in matmul_block; 2) Recip not declared - missing eltwise_math.hpp include

description: Complete rewrite of all kernels + program descriptor. Key issues to fix from prior WIP: (1) Block sizes B_Q_T/B_KV_T are compile-time fixed at 4 — must handle shapes with fewer tiles by making block sizes dynamic via min(4, S_tiles). (2) OperandKind::Block cannot be used with InputLifecycle::Streaming — use Scalar or Col/Row correctly. (3) Scaler tiles need re-push per KV-block (reduce pops them). (4) Phase 11 add must not be in-place on cb_sum_old if it was already popped. (5) compute_kernel_hw_startup needs SrcOrder::Reverse for matmul. (6) Writer total_tiles must match compute output count. (7) m_i/l_i/O_i init must be per-Q-block, not per-kernel.

error: TRISC0 stuck in BinaryFpu wait_per_tile on cb_sum_old(30) or cb_sum_new(29) at line 201 (Phase 11: add<cb_sum_old, cb_sum_new, cb_sum_old>). Writer stuck at cb_wait_front(cb_out). Reader also stuck. The compute kernel is at Phase 11 add — same hang location as the drafter hit.

description: Phase 11 add<cb_sum_old, cb_sum_new, cb_sum_old>(B_q_t) uses default OperandKind::Scalar for both A and B. But cb_sum_old and cb_sum_new are Col-shaped (B_q_t x 1 tiles). OperandKind::Scalar means the chain iterates as 1x1 — only processing 1 tile, then waiting for more from each CB. Since both CBs have exactly B_q_t tiles, and the chain expects 1 tile from each per iteration but loops B_q_t times, this might actually work. BUT: the real issue may be that in-place Streaming on cb_sum_old causes a CB deadlock: the chain pops cb_sum_old tile, then tries to reserve_back on cb_sum_old to push the result, but the CB might be full. Actually, with Streaming input (PerTile wait, PerTile pop) and Streaming output (PerTile reserve, PerTile push), the in-place pattern should work: pop 1, push 1, pop 1, push 1... The CB has B_q_t pages. After phase 7, cb_sum_old has B_q_t tiles. But wait — does the reader push new tiles for cb_sum_old? The reader pushes B_q_t tiles at Q-block init, then phase 7 rescales them in-place. After phase 7, cb_sum_old has B_q_t tiles. Phase 11 add reads cb_sum_old (Streaming pop) + cb_sum_new (Streaming pop), outputs to cb_sum_old (push). This should work with B_q_t pages. Unless the issue is that cb_sum_new does NOT have B_q_t tiles — maybe the SUM reduce with WaitUpfrontNoPop pushed 0 tiles?

error: device timeout, potential hang - cores 24-17, 20-16, 23-16 stuck

symptom: BRISC stuck at cb_wait_front in writer kernel line 24 — compute never pushed to cb_out

description: Study matmul_block helper API

Also need to fix matmul shape params (of() takes 7 args: in0_num_subblocks, in1_num_subblocks, out_subblock_h, out_subblock_w, in0_block_k, num_k_blocks, batch).
